text:
Sofia Martinez: FYI - Ora Systems returned SOW asking for 55% off for first 3 months + Net60 billing and waived onboarding fees. Can we approve?

Ethan Park: Account owned by Ethan (AM). Pilot 3mo, est 40M tokens/month (mix gen + embeddings). Potential Dedicated in H1 next year.

Priya Singh: Finance flag: 55% is outside standard pilot concessions. We usually cap at 35% for new logos unless there is a clear ARR commit. Net60 on a new account increases DSO. Any upstream PO or finance guarantee?

Sofia Martinez: Customer says legal requires Net60. They can issue PO but not a prepay. They're sensitive to onboarding cost; asked for waived $6k setup fee.

Markus Voss: From Revenue Ops perspective, we prefer to keep list price on invoices and apply a contra credit for any one-off onboarding waiver. That preserves revenue recognition. We also need credit checks if Net60.

Priya Singh: Propos...

title: #finance: 1907654321
preview: Ethan Park: Hey @finance — weird one from last night. Took an approved rideshare (Uber) to the airport but the driver canceled and I had to hop in a taxi. I used my personal card for the taxi — taxi gave a non-itemized paper receipt for the fare (€48) and I also tipped the driver cash (€8). The Uber charge later sho...
text:
Ethan Park: Hey @finance — weird one from last night. Took an approved rideshare (Uber) to the airport but the driver canceled and I had to hop in a taxi. I used my personal card for the taxi — taxi gave a non-itemized paper receipt for the fare (€48) and I also tipped the driver cash (€8). The Uber charge later showed up pending on the corp card for €52 (canceled-trip fee) even though I never accepted the Uber. How should I file this? Can I get the taxi + tip reimbursed and the corp card canceled-trip fee refunded?
Maria Gomez: Sorry that happened. Two parts: (1) Re taxi + tip: corporate policy reimburses necessary ground transport when corp card cannot be used or driver unavailable. We need the taxi receipt + a one-line note explaining the corp card attempt. Tips in cash are reimbursable up to company cap (€10 per ride) when incidental to travel; please note who you tipped and why. ...

```
{"id":"INV-2026-00789","customer":"BoxedCo","lines":[{"desc":"promo credit","amount_cents":-120000},{"desc":"tax","amount_cents":24000}],"total_cents":-96000}
```

Lina Gomez: Ack. From the postmortem notes: root cause = retries + missing idempotency on meter-ingest. Fix was merged yesterday but we still need backfill + invoice adjustments.

Lina Gomez: Impact window matches Carlos' range. Estimated affected invoices ~120 customers (mostly mid-size). Lina -> I'll own invoice corrections and c...

title: #finance: 1774506789
preview:
Aisha Noor: FYI Solstice Labs GmbH sent invoice SL-3389 for SaaS support billed to Redwood US and included 19% German VAT but DID NOT list their Umsatzsteuer-ID on the PDF. They also attached a signed W-8BEN-E. Should we hold payment? :eyes:

Ben Carter: German VAT + W-8BEN-E on the same doc feels off. For B2B cross...
text:
Aisha Noor: FYI Solstice Labs GmbH sent invoice SL-3389 for SaaS support billed to Redwood US and included 19% German VAT but DID NOT list their Umsatzsteuer-ID on the PDF. They also attached a signed W-8BEN-E. Should we hold payment? :eyes:

Ben Carter: German VAT + W-8BEN-E on the same doc feels off. For B2B cross-border services to a US legal entity, vendor normally should not charge German VAT (export of services). Also invoice must show supplier VAT ID if they charge VAT.

Lucia Romano: Agreed. W-8BEN-E is for US withholding/tax status of foreign entities and has no bearing on EU VAT. If Solstice is charging VAT they need to include their VAT number (USt-IdNr) and the basis for charging. If the supply is to a US customer, they should either zero-rate or state export of services.

Aisha Noor: I asked them to reissue invoice without VAT or provide a legal basis for charging VAT and...

text:
Laura Bennett: Sharing early shadow-billing compare results (week 1). This is the run that computes both old+new meter outputs but invoices still use current (old) meter. 

Topline from Mei's export (sample: 50 accounts, last 7 days, hosted chat+completions+embeddings):
- Overall delta (new vs old): +0.62% billed_tokens, +0.08% request_count
- P95 account-level delta: +2.4% (two accounts are big outliers)
- Largest negative delta: -3.1% (1 account)

Question: are these expected (token counting alignment) vs bugs (double-count/retries etc)? Need guidance on what we consider acceptable before we expand cohort + turn on alerts.

Mei Lin: Adding more detail. I bucketed deltas by endpoint:
- /v1/chat.completions: +0.4% tokens
- /v1/completions: +0.7% tokens
- /v1/embeddings: +1.9% tokens (but high variance on batched inputs)

Also noticed ~0.3% of usage_events missing region in old pipelin...

title: #finance: 1770061189
preview:
Emily Stone: Need to lock down the usage data freeze window for month-end close. We keep getting late-arriving usage_events and then the ledger shifts after we’ve already started RevRec. Can we agree on a cutoff + handling for late events/backfills?

Mei Lin: +1. Today we’re kind of doing this ad hoc ("don’t rerun a...
text:
Emily Stone: Need to lock down the usage data freeze window for month-end close. We keep getting late-arriving usage_events and then the ledger shifts after we’ve already started RevRec. Can we agree on a cutoff + handling for late events/backfills?

Mei Lin: +1. Today we’re kind of doing this ad hoc ("don’t rerun after X"), which is not great for audit.

Logan Wright: Totally. On the telemetry side we have two times:
- event_time (when request happened)
- ingest_time (when it hit the pipeline)
Late events are usually ingest lag or retries/backfill jobs.

Rishi Malhotra: For margin model + forecasting I also need the month to stop moving under us. Even if we true-up later, I need a stable snapshot for close.

Laura Bennett: We should be explicit: what date/time is the close cutoff, what’s the grace period, and what requires approval. Also: if we find a bug, we don’t want silent change...

Amrita Rao: FYI ClearWave ACH returned (R01). Invoice C-4091 is 37 days outstanding, $12,430. They emailed asking for a short payment plan and to waive the $50 late fee.
Luca Martel: Thanks. I pulled their ledger — steady usage historically, just low this month. Any verbal promises from sales?
Janelle Kim: No promises logged. We sent 2 dunning emails and left a voicemail last week. Customer replied to support earlier today asking if we could waive fee if they pay soon.
Mateo Alvarez: R01 often means wrong routing/account closed. If they can CC-pay now it's safest. CC token for partial capture reduces exposure.
Amrita Rao: Thinking two paths: A) 7-day full pay with $50 waiver, or B) split into 2 installments (50/50) with CC auth for first and ACH retry for second but keep fee. Thoughts?

text:
Rishi Malhotra: Hey all — FP&A ask for the unit economics dashboard: we need **quarter-to-date blended $/1M output tokens** for the exec KPI pack.

Rishi Malhotra: A few questions we need to lock down so we don’t get surprised during close:
1) What’s the definition of **QTD window** (calendar quarter vs fiscal)?
2) What’s the **day boundary / timezone**? We report in PT at day granularity.
3) What’s the **freshness expectation**? If we’re using hourly rollups, when do we consider a day “final”?
4) **Reconciliation expectations**: what % delta vs billing/infra spend is acceptable and where do we document known gaps?

Laura Bennett: +1. We can’t put a metric in the KPI pack unless we can explain deltas. Even if it’s “this is allocation-based, not invoice-based” — needs to be explicit.

Talia Benson: Looping in telemetry + product folks. @Logan Wright @Mei Lin @Nadia Rahman @Marissa Cole...
